text:
What effect does the use of an AI code generator have on the learning, retention, and process of novice programmers learning their first programming language? I experimented this with the OpenAI Codex plugin to Visual Studio Code. The Codex can generate code for a given natural language description of a programming statement. Using this plugin, I conducted a controlled study experiment with 69 novice learners in the age group of 10-17 years. Each learner had to code 45 Python statements. The students were divided into two groups. One group had access to Codex for authoring code for these 45 statements. The other group did not have access to the Codex for these 45 statements. For all 45 statements, all learners had to do the code modification task manually.

Summary of findings: Increased productivity, accuracy and retention of code using Codex Learning to code can be a tedious and time-consuming task for novice learners. Our research explored the impact of using Codex, a code completion AI, on novice learners during a 2-week coding curriculum and their retention post-tests. We found that Novice learners using Codex were able to code faster (1.15x more completions and 1.8x more accurate) without impacting the number of edits needed to produce the final code, which is a key indicator of manual coding productivity. Furthermore, learners that used Codex during the curriculum had a slight, yet not statistically significant, advantage compared to those who did not. Novice learners with higher prior programming ability (measured using a Scratch pre-test) who used Codex had significantly higher retention post-test scores. These results indicate that that an AI-assistant tool, such as Codex, can provide a useful scaffolding tool for novice learners. Additionally, our results do not indicate that such a tool will lead to learners becoming overly reliant on the technology.
